item.title: Google’s Fitbit Air Gives Whoop Some Serious Competition
item.description: The Fitbit Air, a new $100 screenless wearable from Alphabet Inc.’s Google, represents a major evolution in what consumers can expect from fitness trackers as tech companies race into an era of personalized health and artificial intelligence-powered wellness insights.

item.title: ECB to Do ‘Everything in Its Power’ to Tame Inflation: Sleijpen
item.description: The European Central Bank will do whatever is needed to bring consumer-price growth back to target, with officials to have much more information at June’s meeting as they formulate their plans, Governing Council member Olaf Sleijpen said.

item.title: Insurance Underwriter Safepoint Seeks $283.3 Million in US IPO
item.description: Safepoint Holdings Inc., an underwriter of specialty homeowners and commercial insurance, is seeking to raise as much as $283.3 million in an US initial public offering.

item.title: Bessent Has Limited Options to Halt Climb in Treasury Yields
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent is facing what may become his biggest financial-market test yet, in the form of a steady increase in benchmark Treasury yields that’s generating economic headwinds and leaving him with few easy options.

item.title: Goldman Analysts Say UK Homebuilder Selloff Has Gone Too Far
item.description: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. says the selloff in UK homebuilder stocks has been excessive and investors are too pessimistic on the sector’s long-term profitability.

item.title: Kenya Plans $772 Million Green Bonds to Boost Agriculture Output
item.description: Kenya plans 100 billion shillings ($772 million) of green bonds to boost agricultural production, climate resilience and competitiveness.

item.title: PGIM Backs $4 Billion of US Land-Bank Deals in Asset-Based Push
item.description: Prudential Financial Inc.’s asset-management arm has financed about $4 billion of land-banking projects through a partnership with Domain Real Estate Partners, part of a push to gain exposure to the US homebuilding industry.

item.title: Wall Street’s AI Winner Hunt Leads to Seasoning Maker in Japan
item.description: The beneficiaries of the artificial intelligence buildout are spreading far beyond technology high-flyers. Laura Lau found one in seasoning maker Ajinomoto Co.

item.title: Hungary Holds Its Key Interest Rate With June Cut Seen in Play
item.description: Hungary’s central bank kept its key interest rate unchanged at the second-highest level in the European Union, before potentially weighing monetary easing next month.

item.title: Crypto Exchange OKX Makes AI Skills a Part of Worker Evaluations
item.description: Crypto exchange OKX is taking a page from the playbook of the tech and finance industries by linking employee evaluations to proficiency in using artificial intelligence.

item.title: Russia’s Key Black Sea Port Loads Crude After Ukraine’s Attack
item.description: Russia’s key Black Sea oil export terminal, targeted by Ukrainian drones over the weekend, is loading crude, according to satellite data.

item.title: US Premarket Movers for May 26, 2026
item.description: S&P 500 Index futures rise 0.6% as of 7:47 a.m. in New York as investors remained hopeful that the latest American strikes on Iran won’t derail talks to end the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Activist Ancora Pushes H.B. Fuller to Drop Bid for UK’s AMS
item.description: Activist investor Ancora Holdings Group is pushing US adhesives maker H.B. Fuller Co. to abandon a proposed takeover of UK-based Advanced Medical Solutions Group Plc.

item.title: SEBI Plans New Measures to Deepen India’s Corporate Bond Market
item.description: India’s capital markets regulator is working on measures to strengthen the local-currency bond segment as it seeks to cut companies’ reliance on bank loans and support the nation’s push towards a developed economy.

item.title: BP Fires Board Chair on Governance Concerns Only Months Into Job
item.description: BP Plc’s board fired Chairman Albert Manifold just months into the job after serious concerns were raised about governance standards, oversight and conduct.

item.title: Greenbriar-Backed Applied Aerospace Seeks $682.5 Million in IPO
item.description: Applied Aerospace & Defense Inc., a space and defense engineering firm backed by private equity firm Greenbriar Equity Group, is seeking to raise as much as $682.5 million in an initial public offering, as companies in the sector rush to public markets ahead of SpaceX’s potential listing later this year.

item.title: China Coal-Mine Blast Exposes Limits of National Safety Drive
item.description: Overall conditions have improved, but local priorities can stray from Beijing’s grander vision.

item.title: JPMorgan’s Matejka Sees Stocks Overpricing Risk of Rate Hikes
item.description: Markets are overpricing potential central bank interest-rate increases, creating conditions for a rally in stocks with the lowest volatility like staples and utilities, according to strategists at JPMorgan Chase & Co.

item.title: Patrick Drahi Swiss Assets Seized in Spat With Altice Co-Founder
item.description: Patrick Drahi’s spat with Altice co-founder Armando Pereira has escalated after Swiss courts ordered the seizure of assets following a lawsuit by the latter to reclaim at least 1.2 billion francs ($1.5 billion) he claims he’s owed.

item.title: Uzbekistan Resumes Gold Export With $1.5 Billion Through April
item.description: Uzbekistan, one of the world’s largest gold producers, has resumed full scale gold exports in April after a half-year pause.

item.title: There Aren’t Enough GPUs on Earth — Let Alone for Space
item.description: SpaceX’s IPO filing warns that current AI chip supply may be insufficient for its long-term orbital compute ambitions

item.title: Wealth Tax Debate Ignites in UK Leadership Race
item.description: Amid doubts over the UK premier and jittery markets, hopefuls to succeed him are touting tax hikes.

item.title: The Race for US Tariff Refunds Gets Off to a Quiet Start
item.description: More than 330,000 firms paid Trump’s now-illegal tariffs and he’s not happy with those seeking the money back

item.title: Honeywell-Backed Quantinuum Seeks to Raise $1.05 Billion in IPO
item.description: Quantinuum Inc., a quantum computing company backed by Honeywell International Inc., is seeking to raise $1.05 billion in its US initial public offering, capitalizing on investor enthusiasm for the technology.

item.title: Insilico Taps US Partner to Build AI Model for Human Longevity
item.description: Hong Kong AI drug discovery firm Insilico Medicine Cayman TopCo is stepping up the quest to harness artificial intelligence to extend human life.

item.title: Logistics Firm Stord Nabs $250 Million to Help Brands Take On Amazon
item.description: Stord Inc., a logistics technology startup, raised $250 million in a new funding round to expand its fulfillment operation and help merchants better compete with Amazon.com Inc.

item.title: Italy’s Meloni Says EU Bureaucrats Stifle Economic Growth
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni accused the European Union of restricting the bloc’s ability to grow and be competitive on the world stage.

item.title: Kenya in Talks With Karpowership for Emergency Power Supply
item.description: Kenya plans to contract Karpowership, the world’s biggest provider of ship-mounted electricity plants, to provide emergency power for the nation’s growing demands.

item.title: Audemars Piguet CEO Resta Says Swatch Tie-up Smashed Web Records
item.description: For Audemars Piguet Chief Executive Officer Ilaria Resta, the response to the luxury Swiss timepiece maker’s launch of a pocket watch in collaboration with Swatch has been overwhelming.

item.title: Nokia’s 140% Rally Turns AI Comeback Into Valuation Puzzle
item.description: A rally in Nokia Oyj is forcing investors to reconsider the company not as an old telecom-equipment stock, but as part of the infrastructure underpinning the artificial intelligence boom.
